sentences.english: Effective next semester, the Hartwell University Main Library will extend its operating hours on weekdays.
sentences.korean: 다음 학기부터, 하트웰 대학교 메인 도서관이 평일 운영 시간을 연장합니다.

sentences.english: The library will now remain open until midnight, two hours later than the current closing time of 10:00 p.m.
sentences.korean: 도서관은 이제 현재 폐관 시간인 밤 10시보다 2시간 늦은 자정까지 열립니다.

sentences.english: This change was made in response to student requests for more study time during final examination periods.
sentences.korean: 이 변경은 기말 시험 기간 동안 더 많은 공부 시간을 원하는 학생들의 요청에 응답하여 이루어졌습니다.

sentences.english: The extended hours will apply Monday through Thursday.
sentences.korean: 연장된 시간은 월요일부터 목요일까지 적용됩니다.

sentences.english: Friday and weekend hours will remain unchanged.
sentences.korean: 금요일과 주말 시간은 변경 없이 유지됩니다.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: Students are encouraged to use the library's quiet study rooms, which will be available during all operating hours.
sentences.korean: 학생들은 모든 운영 시간 동안 이용 가능한 도서관 조용한 학습실을 이용하시기 바랍니다.

sentences.english: Did you see the notice about the library hours?
sentences.korean: 도서관 시간에 대한 공지 봤어?

sentences.english: Yeah, I just read it.
sentences.korean: 응, 방금 읽었어.

sentences.english: Honestly, I'm pretty happy about it.
sentences.korean: 솔직히, 꽤 기뻐.

sentences.english: Really? Why?
sentences.korean: 정말? 왜?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: Well, I always study late, and the 10 o'clock closing time always felt too early.
sentences.korean: 음, 나는 항상 늦게까지 공부하는데, 10시 폐관은 항상 너무 이르게 느껴졌어.

sentences.english: I'd just be getting into a good focus zone and then have to pack up.
sentences.korean: 막 집중이 잘 되려는데 짐을 싸야 했거든.

sentences.english: Two more hours is going to make a huge difference for me.
sentences.korean: 2시간 더 늘어나면 나한테는 엄청난 차이가 될 거야.

sentences.english: I guess that makes sense.
sentences.korean: 그게 맞는 말인 것 같아.

sentences.english: I usually finish before 10, but I know a lot of people who struggle with that.
sentences.korean: 나는 보통 10시 전에 끝나지만, 그것 때문에 힘들어하는 사람들을 많이 알아.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: Exactly. And they're keeping the quiet rooms open the whole time, which is great.
sentences.korean: 맞아. 그리고 조용한 방도 내내 열어둔대, 그건 좋다.

sentences.english: The dorms get so noisy at night — there's no way I can concentrate there.
sentences.korean: 기숙사는 밤에 너무 시끄러워져서 — 거기서는 도저히 집중할 수가 없어.

sentences.english: That's a good point.
sentences.korean: 좋은 지적이야.

sentences.english: I live off campus, so the commute is my issue.
sentences.korean: 나는 캠퍼스 밖에 살아서, 통학이 문제야.

sentences.english: But I guess if the library is open later, I might be able to stay after my night class instead of driving home and coming back.
sentences.korean: 하지만 도서관이 더 늦게까지 열면, 집에 갔다 다시 오는 대신 야간 수업 후에 머무를 수 있을 것 같아.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: See? It works for more people than you'd think.
sentences.korean: 봐? 생각보다 더 많은 사람들에게 도움이 돼.

sentences.english: I'm glad the university actually listened to the feedback.
sentences.korean: 대학이 실제로 피드백을 들어줘서 기쁘다.

sentences.english: The man expresses his opinion about the change announced in the notice.
sentences.korean: 남자가 공지에서 발표된 변경에 대한 의견을 표현합니다.

sentences.english: State his opinion and explain the reasons he gives for holding that opinion.
sentences.korean: 그의 의견을 진술하고 그 의견을 갖는 이유를 설명하세요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
